evidence: Nine packages map cleanly to domain capabilities: model (data), config (grammar loading), query (corpus search), exploration (semantic field algorithms), api (HTTP handlers), collections (data structures), utils (math/CQL helpers), indexer/blacklab (corpus indexing), viz (SVG output).
model/package-info.java documents the naming convention split between traditional getX accessors and record-style accessors — a small but clear signal of intentional ownership.
ExploreResponseBuilder, ExplorationHandlers, and SketchHandlers each have a single, nameable responsibility that a new engineer can describe in one sentence.
WordSketchApiServer Javadoc lists all endpoints inline — the entry point is self-documenting.

Edge and RelationEdgeType are placed in the exploration package despite being pure presentation types (graph serialization) consumed only by the api layer — this blurs the exploration package's role as a 'computation' package.

SemanticFieldExplorer hosts both single-seed exploration logic and multi-seed exploration logic in one 370-line class; the multi-seed path (exploreMultiSeed) is architecturally a separate operation that shares no phase helpers with the single-seed path.

ComparisonResult is a traditional class (non-record) with mutable-style accessors while peer model types (AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun) are records — minor inconsistency documented in package-info but visible to new contributors.
The ExploreOptions Javadoc explicitly calls out the Explore vs Exploration naming asymmetry as intentional — honest but the asymmetry itself is still a minor readability tax.

strengths: Package names correspond precisely to domain capabilities — a new engineer can predict file location from the domain term alone.
WordSketchApiServer is a pure wiring class with endpoint documentation in its Javadoc; no business logic leaks into the composition root.
model/package-info.java proactively documents cross-cutting naming conventions, signaling intentional architecture stewardship.
ExploreResponseBuilder is a focused, named translator class that keeps model objects free of presentation concerns.
high_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are minor ownership blurriness at specific boundaries (exploration package hosting presentation types; SemanticFieldExplorer hosting two algorithms) rather than systemic decomposition failure.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The high-level structure is domain-aligned and predictable, which earns a solid base. Two things prevent a higher score: Edge and RelationEdgeType live in exploration where they create conceptual noise for any engineer reading the exploration package as 'computation logic', and SemanticFieldExplorer bundles two independently-motivated algorithms (single-seed vs multi-seed) whose helpers do not overlap, making the class's stated responsibility 'analyse semantic fields' a mild abstraction over two distinct operations. Neither issue is a blocker, but they are the type of surprise that a new engineer would encounter on their first code-reading session.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer hosts single-seed and multi-seed algorithms with no shared phase helpers
evidence: exploreByPattern (single-seed) uses four phase helpers: fetchSeedCollocates, buildCollocateToNounsMap, filterCandidates, identifyCoreCollocates — none used by exploreMultiSeed.
exploreMultiSeed (multi-seed) performs its own per-seed collocate fetch, intersection, and CoreCollocate construction inline with no helper extraction.
The two paths differ architecturally: single-seed does a two-hop graph traversal; multi-seed does per-seed individual queries then intersects results.
issues.suggestion: Extract exploreMultiSeed and its inline logic into a MultiSeedExplorer class in the exploration package, mirroring CollocateProfileComparator's extraction. SemanticFieldExplorer becomes a thin facade delegating to both, keeping the public API stable while making each algorithm's ownership clear.

issues.summary: Edge and RelationEdgeType belong to exploration but represent API-layer presentation concerns
evidence: Edge.toMap() serialises to JSON — it is a DTO, not a computation type.
Neither SemanticFieldExplorer nor CollocateProfileComparator instantiates Edge; only ExploreResponseBuilder in the api package does.

The exploration package now contains: computation (SemanticFieldExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator), option bags (ExploreOptions), and presentation DTOs (Edge, RelationEdgeType) — three different responsibilities.
issues.suggestion: Move Edge and RelationEdgeType to the model package, which already holds all other data-transfer types. Update imports in ExploreResponseBuilder and ExplorationHandlers (two callers).

root_causes: Edge and RelationEdgeType were created alongside SemanticFieldExplorer before the model-cycle-fix refactor moved edge construction to ExploreResponseBuilder; the types were never migrated.
exploreMultiSeed was added as an extension to the existing explorer class rather than as a second parallel class, which is a common incremental-growth pattern.
